steps.title: Analyze the sentence structure around the blank
steps.content: Before the blank is an independent clause: "the portraits reveal themselves to be [quilts]". After the blank is a noun phrase with a participial modifier: "the stitching barely visible among the thousands of pieces of printed, microcut fabric." This phrase has no main verb, so it is not an independent clause — it adds descriptive detail about how the quilts appear.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule to eliminate wrong choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘quilts, and the’: a comma plus "and" joins two independent clauses; the second element here is a noun phrase, not an independent clause, so this is incorrect. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘quilts, the’: a comma alone correctly separates an independent clause from a following descriptive noun phrase that elaborates on it. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘quilts; the’: a semicolon must join two independent clauses; using it before a noun phrase is incorrect. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘quilts. The’: a period creates a sentence fragment beginning with "The stitching," since that noun phrase has no main verb. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
